zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Southwick於1568年1月22日出生在英國斯塔福爾特哈爾時，他的父親John Southwick23歲，母親Joan Ann Hill28歲。他於1604年12月10日與Anne Shelley在英國斯塔福爾特哈爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1642年在他的家鄉享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Southwick于1568年1月22日出生在英国斯塔福爾特哈爾时，他的父亲John Southwick23岁，母亲Joan Ann Hill28岁。他于1604年12月10日与Anne Shelley在英国斯塔福爾特哈爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1642年在他的家乡享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Southwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Southwick, including those in Durham, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Northamptonshire, Sussex, and Wiltshire, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + wīc ‘dwelling, specialized farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Southwick was born on 22 January 1568, in Tettenhall, Staffordshire, England, his father, John Southwick, was 23 and his mother, Joan Ann Hill, was 28. He married Anne Shelley on 10 December 1604, in Tettenhall, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1642, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Southwick è nato il il 22 gennaio 1568 nel Tettenhall, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, John Southwick, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Joan Ann Hill, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Anne Shelley il 10 dicembre 1604, nel Tettenhall, Staffordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1642, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Southwick(이)가 1568년 1월 22일 영국 스태퍼드실르 테텐홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Southwick(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Ann Hill(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1604년 12월 10일 영국 스태퍼드실르 테텐홀에서 Anne Shelley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1642년 그의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Southwick nasceu em 22 de janeiro de 1568, em Tettenhall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Southwick, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Joan Ann Hill, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Shelley em 10 de dezembro de 1604, em Tettenhall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1642, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Southwick родился 22 января 1568 г. в местности теттенолл, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Southwick было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Ann Hill, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Shelley 10 декабря 1604 г. в местности теттенолл, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1642 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.
